title: Idiyappam (String Hoppers)
description: is a traditional South Indian (especially Kerala & Tamil Nadu) and Sri Lankan dish made from rice flour

ingredients.title: Ingredients (Serves 4)
items: Rice Flour – 2 cups (fine roasted rice flour; ideally idiyappam flour, freshly ground)
Hot Water – 1 ½ to 2 cups (adjust gradually)
Coconut Oil / Ghee – 1 tsp (for softness & flavor)
Freshly Grated Coconut – ½ cup (for garnish, optional)

instruction.title: 1. Prepare the Dough
steps: Boil water until it reaches a rolling boil.
In a wide bowl, mix rice flour and salt.
Gradually pour hot water into the flour, mixing with a wooden spatula or spoon.
When slightly cooled, knead into a smooth, soft, non-sticky dough.
Add 1 tsp coconut oil to keep it pliable.

instruction.title: 2. Shaping the Idiyappam
steps: Grease the idiyappam press (sev maker with fine perforated plate) with oil.
Press out thin noodles directly onto greased idli plates, banana leaves, or a steamer plate, forming small nests.

instruction.title: 3. Steaming
steps: Steam in an idli steamer for 8–10 minutes until cooked and fluffy.
Do not over-steam, as strands may turn hard.

instruction.title: 4. Finishing Touch
steps: Sprinkle freshly grated coconut on top (traditional Kerala style).
For restaurant presentation, drizzle with ghee for aroma.

instruction.title: 5. Suggested Pairings
steps: Savory: Vegetable stew, Kerala egg curry, or chicken curry
Luxury Touch: Infused coconut milk with cardamom & saffron drizzle

chefTips: Use idiyappam flour (rice roasted and ground extra fine) instead of regular rice flour.
Always use boiling hot water; lukewarm water won’t bind properly.
Knead quickly but gently – over-kneading makes the idiyappam dense.
For an elegant plating, twist idiyappam strands into neat bird’s nest–style spirals.
Serve immediately; idiyappam tends to dry out if left uncovered.
